sections.content:
LEVEL 1 COMPLETED! The key was:
1. Make 4 cross cells green
2. Press LEFT repeatedly until the right bar blocks merge with the left bar blocks
Now I'm on Level 2. Let me analyze the new board:
- The bar at the top now has a vertical structure with blue/sky blue blocks at top and bottom
- The left panel now has 3 yellow dots in an L pattern
- The grid has 4 white cross cells, corners and center gray
- The right edge still has the white strip
I need to understand the new level's mechanics. Let me first inspect the board structure.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Inspect Level 2 structure
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Level 2 objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    bnd = n['boundary']
    r = sum(p[0] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
    c = sum(p[1] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
    print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({r},{c}), hash={n['hash'][:8]}")
print(f"\nValid actions: {valid_actions}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try pressing UP to see what happens to the bar blocks
result = action(['UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After UP:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('b', 'S') and n['pixels'] <= 20:
        bnd = n['boundary']
        r = sum(p[0] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        c = sum(p[1] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({r},{c})")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

# Let me also check grid cells
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 9:
        bnd = n['boundary']
        r = sum(p[0] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        c = sum(p[1] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        print(f"  Grid ({r},{c}): color={n['color']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
